LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFFS ROBERT STEELE AND ORLANDO NARANJO'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES, COSTS AND CLASS REPRESENTATIVE ENHANCEMENT PAYMENTS. THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED REGARDING ATTORNEYS' FEES WHICH THE COURT FINDS TOO HIGH. THE MULTIPLIER "PREMIUM" REQUESTED IS NOT JUSTIFIED BY THE NATURE OF THIS CASE, WHICH IS OF A FAIRLY ROUTINE TYPE. PLAINTIFFS ROBERT STEELE AND ORLANDO NARANJO'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES, COSTS AND CLASS REPRESENTATIVE ENHANCEMENT PAYMENTS IS GRANTED WHICH IS BASED ON CALCULATING 25% OF THE SETTLEMENT FUND PLUS $60,000.00 AND COSTS. MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED REGARDING ATTORNEYS' FEES WHICH THE COURT FINDS TOO HIGH. THE MULTIPLIER "PREMIUM" REQUESTED IS NOT JUSTIFIED BY THE NATURE OF THIS CASE, WHICH IS OF A FAIRLY ROUTINE TYPE. PLAINTIFFS ROBERT STEELE AND ORLANDO NARANJO'S Motion For Attorneys' Fees, Costs And Class Representative Enhancement Payments IS GRANTED WHICH IS BASED ON CALCULATING 25% OF THE SETTLEMENT FUND PLUS $60,000.00 AND COSTS. Final Approval Hearing
Off Calendar Oct-01-2014 Continued To Oct-23-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RAUL PEREZ OF THE CAPSTONE LAW, A.P.C., TEL. NO. (3100 556-4811 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. NATHAN W. AUSTIN OF JACKSON LEWIS, TEL. NO. (916)341-0404 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDAN ZUMEZ INC. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED REGARDING FEES. Final Approval Hearing is continued to October 23, 2014. Moving party shall file a supplemental brief with the Court no less than five court days prior to the new hearing date, addressing the following items: 1) the total number of participating class members; 2) the total number of participating class members in each subclass; 3) the aggregate number of workweeks of all class members; 4) the total number of workweeks of each subclass; 5) the number of weeks for which each individual class member qualifies; 6) the rate at which workweeks shall be reimbursed ("Workweek Value") for each subclass. The above should provide the formulas explaining the dollar values of each workweek for the two subclasses (e.g., "Class 1 is to be paid $70 per week, Class 2 is to be paid $90 per week."). Further, the disposition of uncashed checks leftover from the Net Settlement Amount should be clarified. The cy pres organization, the Zumiez Foundation, appears to be designated to receive uncashed settlement checks. The bona fides of the Zumiez Foundation should be explained. It appears to have close ties to the Defendant and the Court questions its benefits to a charitable cause-does it benefit Sumiez and its employees? Attorneys fees should be limited to 25%, thus 75% will be paid to the class less out of pocket expenses. Class counsel did not provide hourly rates or hours spent on this case. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorneys' Fees, Costs And Class Representative Enhancement Payments
Off Calendar Oct-01-2014 Continued To Oct-23-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RAUL PEREZ OF THE CAPSTONE LAW, A.P.C., TEL. NO. (3100 556-4811 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. NATHAN W. AUSTIN OF JACKSON LEWIS, TEL. NO. (916)341-0404 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ZUMEZ INC. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED REGARDING FEES. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFs ROBERT STEELE and ORLANDO NARANJO's Motion For Attorneys' Fees, Costs And Class Representative Enhancement Payments is continued to October 23, 2014. Moving party shall file a supplemental brief with the Court no less than five court days prior to the new hearing date, addressing the following items: 1) the total number of participating class members; 2) the total number of participating class members in each subclass; 3) the aggregate number of workweeks of all class members; 4) the total number of workweeks of each subclass; 5) the number of weeks for which each individual class member qualifies; 6) the rate at which workweeks shall be reimbursed ("Workweek Value") for each subclass. The above should provide the formulas explaining the dollar values of each workweek for the two subclasses (e.g., "Class 1 is to be paid $70 per week, Class 2 is to be paid $90 per week."). Further, the disposition of uncashed checks leftover from the Net Settlement Amount should be clarified. The cy pres organization, the Zumiez Foundation, appears to be designated to receive uncashed settlement checks. The bona fides of the Zumiez Foundation should be explained. It appears to have close ties to the Defendant and the Court questions its benefits to a charitable cause-does it benefit Sumiez and its employees? Attorneys fees should be limited to 25%, thus 75% will be paid to the class less out of pocket expenses. Class counsel did not provide hourly rates or hours spent on this case. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
